rationale: Wrap the determinism obligation in `assert(A ==> det_set_equal(...)) by { if A { ... } }` so the post1_/post2_ ensures become hypotheses inside the `if`-branch (a bare `assert(post1_self_.seq@ =~= ...)` at the body top is unprovable from the requires alone — this is what produced the `unknown`). Inside the branch, re-state both `=~=` ensures so their Seq-extensionality triggers fire on the shared term `pre_self_.seq@.update(i as int, out)`, chain transitively to `post1_self_.seq@ =~= post2_self_.seq@`, then lift to `==` of the underlying `Seq<A>` and the `Ghost<Seq<A>>` `seq` field; a pointwise `assert forall|j| ...` re-instantiates both quantifiers on the same skolem `j`. `r1 == r2` is trivial since both are `()`.
